behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model-output quality from a real speech-to-text/diarization model; stubbed transcripts would hide mislabeled or dropped speakers (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets one model-output property (speaker labels), not a required multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production speech-to-text API and assert the produced transcript retains correct speaker labels per utterance (or per turn) before summary generation consumes it.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is parser/transformation logic on transcript text to internal structure; real failure can be caught at one function/object boundary with crafted transcript fixtures (MOCK-MISS says this is direct logic). Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff beyond parser output.
behaviors.test_description: Feed transcript snippets with explicit assignments into the parsing function and assert extracted records contain correct assignee and task text fields, including edge phrasing/punctuation cases.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM summarization decisions (coverage/completeness per attendee); deterministic stubs would always pass and miss omission failures (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected Workflow because this behavior validates one live summary quality criterion, not multiple required agentic decision stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use real meeting transcript input with known attendee assignments, call the real summarization model, and assert each attendee with an assigned task appears in at least one generated action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic routing/schema-mapping logic once decisions are already identified; one local transformation boundary is sufficient. Rejected Integration because behavior text says 'identified decisions' and focuses on routing, disqualifying need for module interaction or real infra.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke decision-routing logic with pre-identified decision objects and assert output populates only the `decisions` schema fields and not narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Behavior tests whether model places semantically important content in the right section; this depends on real LLM judgment and can vary across runs (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because the behavior text asserts a single output-placement quality check, not a multi-step agent pipeline requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Submit transcripts containing explicit decisions to the real summarization model and assert those decisions are present in the generated `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Requires chained processing across segments with repeated model calls and merge orchestration; failures emerge from multi-step agentic handoff/order/state, not a single boundary (MULTI-STEP, INTEGRATION-BRAKE d). Rejected Agentic because behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-segment processing plus merge.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through the full segmented path; verify chunking threshold trigger, sequential segment processing, merged output integrity, and cross-segment coherence in final structured summary.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end pipeline validation with multiple dependent model-driven stages (transcription then summarization) and delivery; minimum failure boundary is whole workflow. Rejected System because behavior text includes agentic content correctness stages, making plain infra-only validation insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like end-to-end run on a real meeting file from upload through output delivery, asserting successful unattended completion and valid structured payload with required sections.
